title: Crystal Language Cheat Sheet
description: Quick reference for Crystal programming language syntax and commands.

commands.command:
class Person
  def initialize(@name : String, @age : Int32)
  end
end
commands.description: Define a simple class with an initializer
commands.scenario: Define a simple class with an initializer

commands.command:
def add(a : Int32, b : Int32) : Int32
  a + b
end
commands.description: Create a method with a return type
commands.scenario: Create a method with a return type

commands.command:
if age >= 18
  puts "Adult"
else
  puts "Minor"
end
commands.description: Conditional statement example

commands.command:
names = ["Alice", "Bob", "Charlie"]
names.each do |name|
  puts name
end
commands.description: Create and iterate over an array
commands.scenario: Create and iterate over an array

commands.description: Create a hash and access its values
commands.scenario: Create a hash and access its values

commands.description: Use a tuple to store multiple values
commands.scenario: Use a tuple to store multiple values

commands.command:
spawn do
  puts "Hello from a fiber"
end
commands.description: Spawn a new fiber

commands.command:
channel = Channel(Int32).new
spawn do
  channel.send(42)
end
puts channel.receive
commands.description: Use channels for communication

commands.command:
ch1 = Channel(Int32).new
ch2 = Channel(String).new
select
when value = ch1.receive
  puts value
when message = ch2.receive
  puts message
end
commands.description: Use select to handle multiple channels
commands.scenario: Use select to handle multiple channels

commands.description: Compile a Crystal program
Run a Crystal program directly
Format Crystal code
Raise an exception

commands.command:
begin
  # code that might raise an exception
rescue ex : Exception
  puts ex.message
end
commands.description: Rescue an exception

commands.command:
begin
  # code that might raise an exception
ensure
  puts "This will always run"
end
commands.description: Ensure block execution
Compiles the program with optimizations enabled, significantly improving execution speed and reducing binary size.
Compiles the program with debug symbols and disables optimizations, making it easier to use debuggers like GDB or LLDB.
Generates a detailed report of the current Crystal installation, including compiler version, target architecture, and build settings.
Defines a namespace to group related classes, modules, and methods, preventing name collisions in larger projects.
Loads the standard library module for JSON parsing and serialization, essential for web APIs and data processing.
